title: Rubus tereticaulis microgene subalpinus
intro: Rubus tereticaulis microgene subalpinus, commonly referred to as the subalpine Himalayan raspberry, is a type of plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Rubus tereticaulis microgene subalpinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits and treat various health conditions.
